data.title: Bybit confirms withdrawals are active, warns of delays
data.description:
In a livestream, Bybit CEO stated that 70% of transactions have successfully gone through, though some users may experience delays due to network congestion.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bybit-confirms-withdrawals-active-warns-delays?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: KuCoin CEO: We have started to assist in monitoring the flow of funds and freezing suspicious assets
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Regarding the security incident encountered by Bybit, KuCoin CEO BC Wong expressed support for Bybit and has started to assist in monitoring the flow of funds and freezing suspicious assets. KuCoin always puts the security of user assets first, and continues to strengthen security protection to maintain industry stability.

data.title: Bybit CEO Addresses Security Breach Involving Ethereum Cold Wallet
data.description:
According to Odaily, Bybit CEO recently discussed a security breach during a live broadcast. He explained that Bybit conducts internal asset transfers approximately every three weeks, depending on the balance in their hot wallet. Transfers occur when the hot wallet reaches a threshold deemed necessary for replenishment. The CEO is the final signatory for these transactions, reviewing links, user interface, target addresses, and code, although not thoroughly. 

The breach involved hackers manipulating the user interface on all signatories' computers. It was later confirmed that the attack targeted an Ethereum cold wallet under the secure wallet category, while other cold wallets remained unaffected. The CEO assured that Bitcoin, which constitutes their primary reserve, remains secure.

data.title: Bybit derivatives and institutional head: The platform's P2P function is operating normally
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit's head of derivatives and institutions said in a live broadcast: The platform's P2P function is currently operating normally.

data.title: Beosin: The transaction fee funds of the initial attack address launched by Bybit hackers came from Binance
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Security team Beosin said that after analyzing the address from which the Bybit hacker launched the initial attack, it was found that the handling fee funds of the address came from Binance. The corresponding 4 Binance exchange withdrawal transaction hashes are: 0x64953fc1432bf106f5e8d6b0927a39130865fec013d8403bba8fc4382515884c 0xb9f9e43dc23bdb7b231925dc01e828990d3f84b8ad3305e83ffb6848711f871c 0xd6d871deece52f15e3f2c523dffad4b85c63125d72e4de702445a654de5ce100 0x0afa81cc9c0b0bfc4a9cd46c33bcdecf58199513e7c051e5a9df1617c211f69f

data.title: Bybit CEO: Withdrawals will not be stopped, all products and services will operate as usual
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit CEO Ben Zhou said in a live broadcast, "All products and services are running as usual. We will not stop withdrawals at this time, and we are still processing withdrawals."

data.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Drops Below 97,000 USDT with a 0.92% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin (BTC) dropped below 97,000 USDT and is now trading at 96,973 USDT, with a 0.92% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Bybit Update: Bybit CEO Goes Live to Address $1.44 Billion ETH Theft Incident
data.description:
In response to the massive $1.44 billion security breach, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou has launched a live broadcast on the Bybit platform, providing further insights into the ETH asset theft that has rocked the exchange.According to previous reports, Bybit's multi-signature cold wallet was compromised, leading to unauthorized withdrawals of over 500,000 ETH-related assets, including:401,347 ETH ($1.12...
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/20617056326209?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: ByBit CEO: There Was a Problem with Multi-Signature Transfers but I Didn’t Notice it
data.description: Bybit CEO Ben Zhou revealed in a live broadcast on February 22 that a multi-signature transfer issue contributed to the $1.44 billion ETH theft. Zhou admitted that he was the last to sign the transaction using a Ledger device but failed to notice a critical problem—the delivery address was not displayed correctly during the signing process.As a result of the breach, 4,000 withdrawal applications are currently waiting for processing, highlighting the disruption to Bybit’s operations following the attack.
